blockingIssues.description: Commit df33d5a only updates the timer-page week-range button selector. It does not touch the assigned editor popover path in `apps/website/e2e/time-entry-editor.real-runtime.spec.ts` or the editor UI code, so this feature does not implement the requested `Edit start time` click-interception fix.

The assigned failing interaction remains unchanged in this feature commit and the handoff attributes success to an unrelated selector repair. Passing reruns are not sufficient evidence for a flaky-fix feature when the cited commit contains no stabilization for the editor-popover interaction.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The `frontend-test-worker` guidance still allowed a rerun-only handoff for a flaky-fix feature even though the cited commit did not contain the scoped stabilization. This feature repeated the previously observed traceability gap: the worker claimed the editor-popover fix was complete while landing an unrelated timer-page selector change.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `.factory/skills/frontend-test-worker/SKILL.md` says the handoff commit must directly contain the scoped fix, but transcript session `add63759-b8fa-4477-9812-a17a91cb18c0` ends with commit `df33d5a`; `git show --stat df33d5a` changes only `apps/website/e2e/timer-page.spec.ts` while the assigned feature targets `apps/website/e2e/time-entry-editor.real-runtime.spec.ts`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The tracking-frontend-worker skill instructs workers to read repo-local library files under /Users/opentoggl-ci/Code/opentoggl/.factory/library/, but the worker handoff for this feature marked followedProcedure true without doing the required agent-browser replay and with no interactive checks. The skill should make the manual browser proof requirement enforceable for already-fixed/no-code features or allow an explicit no-op exception.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: tracking-frontend-worker SKILL.md step 11 requires agent-browser replay unless the feature is purely non-visual; handoff 2026-03-25T10-54-38-596Z__tracking-ui-week-range-button-visibility-fix__484289d8-1cc2-45f6-a999-eb53b2bec5a5.json reports interactiveChecks: [] and skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true.

override.reason: Current validator evidence is green: `vp run test -r` passes, including the affected editor and timer-page specs, and `vp run check -r` passes. The remaining scrutiny failure is traceability-only because the milestone resolved a repo-red condition without needing a new scoped product change, so there is no live runtime or assertion gap left to fix in `tracking-ui-followup-3`.
